title: Puerto Banus Catamaran Hire: Hourly Rates and 2026 Guide
html:
If you are looking to rent a catamaran by the hour in Puerto Banus, prices start at around 350 EUR for a two-hour trip departing from the main harbour. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a straightforward booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran hourly in Puerto Banus?

Hourly catamaran hire in Puerto Banus typically ranges from 150 to 400 EUR per hour depending on boat size and season. You can book shorter trips of two to four hours or full-day charters. For a complete price breakdown, explore day charters in Puerto Banus on the BoatHire24 platform, where owners list current rates.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The prime departure point is Puerto Banus marina itself, situated in the heart of Marbella. From here, you can head west towards the port of Estepona or east to the glamorous Marbella Bay. A popular short cruise goes to Cabopino beach and the nearby sand dunes. For a longer day, sail south-west to the bay of Gibraltar or anchor off the coast of San Pedro Alcántara. Each route offers varied coastal views and swimming spots.

Which boat to rent

  1. Lagoon 42 catamaran, ideal for up to 12 guests on a half-day cruise, from 250 EUR per hour.
  2. Azimut 55 motor yacht, suited for a private luxury tour with 14 guests, from 350 EUR per hour.
  3. Shibuli Leopard 40, a versatile catamaran for family hires, from 200 EUR per hour. See the Shibuli charter page for full specs and availability.
  4. Sunseeker Manhattan 50, perfect for entertaining up to 16 guests, from 400 EUR per hour.

Cost breakdown for boat hire in Puerto Banus

  • Shared group tour per person: 80, 150 EUR for a three-hour trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day: 300, 500 EUR for a 4-hour rental.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 1,200, 2,500 EUR depending on size.
  • Crewed yacht with skipper and fuel included: 2,000, 4,500 EUR per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Most hourly catamaran hires in Puerto Banus include a professional skipper, so no special licence is required. If you want to drive the boat yourself, a valid ICC or Spanish nautical licence is mandatory. The operator on BoatHire24 will confirm the exact requirements before booking.

High season runs from June to August, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon. Spring and autumn offer calmer seas and fewer crowds. A local tip:

For a smoother ride, book morning departures before 11am while the sea breeze is still gentle around the Costa del Sol.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150, 400 EUR per hour (catamaran)
  • Peak months: June to August
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: up to 16 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I book a catamaran for just two hours in Puerto Banus?

Yes, many owners offer two-hour minimum charters at a fixed hourly rate, usually starting at 300 EUR.

What is included in the hourly price?

Typically the price includes the boat, fuel, a professional skipper, and basic safety equipment; extra costs may apply for food or drinks.

Are there any age restrictions for passengers?

Children of all ages are welcome on most boats, but life jackets must be worn as required by Spanish maritime law.

Can I cancel my hourly booking and get a refund?

Each listing on BoatHire24 states its own cancellation policy, so check the terms before confirming your reservation.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. In 2026, hourly catamaran hire continues to be a popular way for visitors to explore the Costa del Sol coastline. To browse all options or become a host yourself, visit the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
